title: Uncarina leptocarpa
taxonomy: Uncarina leptocarpa is classified as a plant species in the family Pedaliaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the sesame or leadwort family. This family is native to Africa and includes several species of plants that are often found in dry or woodland environments. The genus Uncarina is a small group of plants that are native to Madagascar, an island country located off the coast of East Africa. The classification of Uncarina leptocarpa places it within the kingdom Plantae, with the rank of species being the lowest taxonomic rank. Further classification includes the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiosperms, class Magnoliopsida, and order Lamiales.
